title: The Song Of The Secret
content:
Where is beauty?
         Gone, gone:
    The cold winds have taken it
        With their faint moan;
    The white stars have shaken it,
        Trembling down,
    Into the pathless deeps of the sea.
         Gone, gone
        Is beauty from me.

    The clear naked flower
        Is faded and dead;
    The green-leafed willow,
        Drooping her head,
    Whispers low to the shade
        Of her boughs in the stream,
         Sighing a beauty,
         Secret as dream.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Walter De La Mare delivers a powerful performance in "The Song Of The Secret"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
